bug_report.summary: `display/display_server/driver.linuxbsd` dropdown popup becomes oversized/wider after making enum non-editable
steps_to_reproduce: Navigate via category tree to Display → Display Server
Locate `display/display_server/driver.linuxbsd` and open its value dropdown
expected_behaviors: Dropdown popup size/width should remain consistent with the prior UI (or be content-fit/consistent with other enum dropdowns), without excessive empty space.
actual_behaviors: Dropdown popup appears larger/wider than before (noticeable empty space and different geometry), indicating an unintended layout/size change beyond just making the enum non-editable.

false_positive_reasons: LLM hallucination — misinterpreted or imagined GUI rendering

bug_report.summary: Project Settings search for `display/display_server/driver.linuxbsd` shows no results (setting not visible/accessible)
steps_to_reproduce: Open the project in the editor.
Navigate to Display → Display Server (if not already selected).
expected_behaviors: The `display/display_server/driver.linuxbsd` project setting is shown in the results list.
The setting can be edited via a strict dropdown/selector (non-editable enum), allowing the user to pick a valid value (e.g., `x11`).
actual_behaviors: The main Project Settings panel is empty (no settings/results shown), so `display/display_server/driver.linuxbsd` cannot be found or changed via the UI.
